title: Bridge Essay: Hyperconnectivity in perisylvian language pathways in schizophrenia with auditory verbal hallucinations: A multi-site diffusion MRI study.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Daimon** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Daimonic voice — voice-hearing continuum, inner speech misattribution, corollary discharge.
**Phase context:** Voice-hearing as spectrum phenomenon — auditory verbal hallucinations, agency detection, inner speech.

**Hyperconnectivity in perisylvian language pathways in schizophrenia with auditory verbal hallucinations: A multi-site diffusion MRI study.** — Schizophr Res (2019). DOI: 10.1016/j.schres.2018.12.024. PMID: 30587426. Auditory verbal hallucinations (AVH) are one of the cardinal symptoms of schizophrenia, and are proposed to be associated with altered integrity of the left perisylvian language pathways. There is considerable heterogeneity in the pattern of white matter abnormalities across previous studies. We investigated the white matter integrity of the perisylvian language pathways in schizophrenia patients with AVH based on a relatively large sample dataset from four different sites. 113 schizophrenia pat
